description: Translation termination in E. coli occurs when the ribosome encounters a stop codon (UAA, UAG, or UGA) in the A site. Class I release factors RF1 (recognizes UAA/UAG) or RF2 (recognizes UAA/UGA) bind to the A site, enter the peptidyl transferase center, and catalyze hydrolysis of the peptidyl-tRNA ester bond, releasing the completed polypeptide. Class II release factor RF3-GTP then promotes dissociation of RF1/RF2. The ribosome recycling factor (RRF) and EF-G cooperate to dissociate the 70S ribosome into 50S and 30S subunits, which are then cleared of deacylated tRNA and mRNA by IF3. This flowchart illustrates the complete termination and recycling pathway from stop codon recognition through ribosome subunit dissociation and preparation for the next round of translation initiation.
mermaid:
graph TD
    Start[/(Translation Termination)/] --> A1[/Ribosome reaches stop codon in A site/]
    
    A1 --> A2{Which stop codon?}
    A2 -->|UAA| A3[UAA recognized by RF1 or RF2]
    A2 -->|UAG amber| A4[UAG recognized by RF1 only]
    A2 -->|UGA opal| A5[UGA recognized by RF2 only]
    
    A3 --> A6{RF1 or RF2 available?}
    A4 --> A7[RF1 binds to ribosome A site]
    A5 --> A8[RF2 binds to ribosome A site]
    
    A6 -->|RF1 binds first| A7
    A6 -->|RF2 binds first| A8
    
    A7 --> A9[/RF1 tripeptide motif PVT recognizes stop codon/]
    A8 --> A10[/RF2 tripeptide motif SPF recognizes stop codon/]
    
    A9 --> A11[RF1 GGQ motif enters peptidyl transferase center PTC]
    A10 --> A12[RF2 GGQ motif enters PTC]
    
    A11 --> A13[GGQ glutamine positioned for catalysis]
    A12 --> A13
    
    A13 --> A14[Water molecule activated in PTC]
    A14 --> A15[Nucleophilic attack on peptidyl-tRNA ester bond]
    A15 --> A16[Peptidyl-tRNA ester bond hydrolyzed]
    
    A16 --> A17[Completed polypeptide released with C-terminal carboxyl]
    A17 --> A18[Deacylated tRNA remains in P site]
    A18 --> A19[RF1 or RF2 remains in A site]
    
    A17 --> A20[Polypeptide exits through ribosome tunnel]
    A20 --> A21[Polypeptide folding or targeting]
    
    A19 --> A22{RF3-GTP available?}
    A22 -->|Yes| A23[RF3-GTP binds to ribosome]
    A22 -->|No| Wait1[Wait for RF3-GTP]
    Wait1 --> A22
    
    A23 --> A24[RF3 GTPase activated]
    A24 --> A25[GTP hydrolysis by RF3]
    A25 --> A26[Conformational change promotes RF1/RF2 dissociation]
    A26 --> A27[RF1 or RF2 released]
    A27 --> A28[RF3-GDP dissociates]
    A28 --> A29[RF3-GTP regenerated]
    
    A27 --> A30["/Post-termination complex: 70S, mRNA, deacylated tRNA/"]
    
    A30 --> A31{RRF available?}
    A31 -->|Yes| A32[Ribosome Recycling Factor RRF binds]
    A31 -->|No| Wait2[Wait for RRF]
    Wait2 --> A31
    
    A32 --> A33[RRF binds to ribosome A site mimicking tRNA L-shape]
    
    A33 --> A34{EF-G-GTP available?}
    A34 -->|Yes| A35[EF-G-GTP binds]
    A34 -->|No| Wait3[Wait for EF-G-GTP]
    Wait3 --> A34
    
    A35 --> A36[EF-G GTPase activated]
    A36 --> A37[GTP hydrolysis by EF-G]
    A37 --> A38[EF-G and RRF cooperate]
    
    A38 --> A39[50S subunit dissociates from 30S]
    A39 --> A40[70S ribosome splits into subunits]
    
    A40 --> A41[RRF dissociates]
    A40 --> A42[EF-G-GDP dissociates]
    
    A40 --> A43["30S subunit retains: mRNA + deacylated tRNA"]
    
    A43 --> A44{IF3 available?}
    A44 -->|Yes| A45[IF3 binds to 30S subunit]
    A44 -->|No| Wait4[Wait for IF3]
    Wait4 --> A44
    
    A45 --> A46[/IF3 prevents premature 50S rejoining/]
    A46 --> A47[IF3 promotes deacylated tRNA dissociation]
    A47 --> A48[tRNA released from P site]
    
    A46 --> A49[IF3 promotes mRNA dissociation]
    A49 --> A50[mRNA released from 30S]
    
    A48 --> A51[Clean 30S subunit with IF3 bound]
    A50 --> A51
    
    A51 --> A52[30S ready for new initiation cycle]
    
    A40 --> A53[Clean 50S subunit]
    A53 --> A54[50S ready for new initiation cycle]
    
    A52 --> End1([Subunits recycled for new translation])
    A54 --> End1
    
    A21 --> End2([Completed polypeptide])

name: Translation Termination and Ribosome Recycling
notes: Translation termination must be highly accurate to prevent readthrough at stop codons while allowing suppressor tRNAs to function when appropriate. RF1 and RF2 have overlapping specificity at UAA but distinct recognition of UAG vs UGA. The ribosome recycling pathway is essential to prevent ribosome depletion and is coordinated with the next initiation event. Recent structures reveal how the GGQ motif positions a water molecule for peptide release, and how RRF mimics tRNA structure to facilitate subunit splitting. This process is a target for translational readthrough drugs in genetic diseases.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on structural and biochemical studies of bacterial translation termination. All release factors (RF1, RF2, RF3), ribosome recycling components (RRF, EF-G, IF3), and catalytic mechanisms have been validated through crystallography and cryo-EM. The model represents the current understanding of how bacteria achieve accurate termination at all three stop codons and efficiently recycle ribosomal subunits for new rounds of translation.
